channel.title: PORID — OR Intelligence Feed
channel.description: Latest publications in Operations Research — aggregated from arXiv, Crossref, OpenAlex, and Semantic Scholar.

item.title: S-shaped Utility Maximization with VaR Constraint and Partial Information
item.description:
Zhu, D., Davey, A., Zheng, H.
Abstract We study S-shaped utility maximisation with VaR constraint and unobservable drift coefficient. Using the Bayesian filter, the concavification principle, and the change of measure, we give a semi-closed integral representation for the dual value function and find a critical wealth level that determines if the constrained problem admits a unique optimal solution and Lagrange multiplier or is infeasible. We also propose three algorithms (Lagrange, simulation, deep neural
item.source: Journal of Optimization Theory and Applications

item.title: Convexification of Fractional Distributionally Robust Probability Maximization Problems under Phi-Divergence Ambiguity
item.description: Nguyen, H., Lejeune, M.
item.source: Journal of Optimization Theory and Applications

item.title: Second-Order Optimality Conditions for Sparse Optimization via Fréchet Second-Order Subdifferential
item.description: Chen, J., Wang, Y., Lv, Y., Yao, J.
item.source: Journal of Optimization Theory and Applications

item.title: Cyclic Relaxed Douglas-Rachford Splitting for Inconsistent Nonconvex Feasibility
item.description:
Dinh, T., Jansen, G., Luke, D.
Abstract
We study the cyclic relaxed Douglas-Rachford algorithm for possibly nonconvex, and inconsistent feasibility problems. This algorithm can be viewed as a convex relaxation between the cyclic Douglas-Rachford algorithm first introduced by Borwein and Tam in 2014 and the classical cyclic projections algorithm. We characterize the fixed points of the cyclic relaxed Douglas-Rachford algorithm and show the relation of the shadows
item.source: Journal of Optimization Theory and Applications

item.title: Explicit Solution to an Optimal Two-player Switching Game in Infinite Horizon
item.description: Asri Brahim, E., Paka, M.
item.source: Journal of Optimization Theory and Applications

item.title: On the Hardness of the $$L_{1}-L_{2}$$ Regularization Problem
item.description: Ouyang, Y., Yates, K.
item.source: Journal of Optimization Theory and Applications

item.title: Reserve Constrained AC Optimal Power Flow Using Accelerated Primal-Dual Interior Point Method
item.description:
Yehong Peng, Yong Fu
The reserve constrained AC optimal power flow (RCOPF) problem integrates operating reserves with system operation scheduling to provide optimal power generation, delivery, as well as reserve allocation. As the reserve functions as a backup capacity for maintaining the balance between electricity supply and demand, co-optimizing both energy and reserve quantities will ensure a higher operational efficiency and reliability of power system. The complex RCOPF problem is characterized by its expanded

item.title: Dynamic Network Usage Fee for Joint Kilowatt and Negawatt Peer-to-Peer Energy Market Based on Knowledge Distillation
item.description:
Yuanxing Xia, Ke Wang, H. Ji, Qi Wang, Linjun Shi et al.
Existing research on joint kilowatt-negawatt peer-to-peer (P2P) electricity markets predominantly relies on iterative coordination between prosumers and the distribution system operator (DSO). This not only undermines real-time market efficiency but also compromises prosumers’ privacy. Such drawbacks pose significant barriers to the practical deployment of P2P markets, where fast response and strict privacy protection are critical requirements. Therefore, there is a pressing need for a market-cl

item.title: Current Optimization for Nonunity Power Factor and Unbalanced DC-Link Voltage in Three-Phase Vienna Rectifiers
item.description:
Yi-Hung Liao, Jia-Sheng Liu
The suppression of current zero-crossing distortion and dc-link voltage oscillations has been a key research focus for three-phase Vienna rectifiers in recent years. In addition, under variable power factor (PF) operation, the phase difference between the input current and grid voltage increases the likelihood of the Vienna rectifier entering a nonsynthetic interval at a high modulation index. This prevents the effective synthesis of the reference voltage vector, leading to severe current distor

item.title: Co-evolution with hierarchical decomposition for Vehicle Routing Problem with Drones
item.description: Ruonan Zhai, Xuejun Zhang, Yi Mei, Tong Guo, Wenbo Du

item.title: Bidirectional energy supply logistics using uncrewed electric aerial and ground vehicles: A two-echelon location-routing problem with resource-constrained demand allocation and time windows
item.description: Hyunhwa Kim, Denissa Sari Darmawi Purba, Eleftheria Kontou

item.title: Deep reinforcement learning approach to solving clustered vehicle routing problems
item.description: Yaoxin Wu, Yueting Yu, Lingxiao Wu, Tao Feng, Lu Zhang et al.

item.title: LLM-based automatic heuristic design for vehicle-drone collaborative routing problems
item.description: Haiyang Shi, Lu Zhen

item.title: The logistics vehicle charging station location selection and routing problem with partial recharging and shared fleets
item.description: Yong Wang, Shiqi Zhang, Jing Liu, Yuanhan Wei, Haizhong Wang

item.title: A Hybrid Graph-Quantum Neural Network for Solar Irradiance Forecasting Using Chaotic Swarm Optimization and Cauchy Distribution
item.description:
Ying-Yi Hong, Christian Lian Paulo P. Rioflorido, Yun-Yuan Wang
Solar and photovoltaic power operations rely on accurate Global Horizontal Irradiance (GHI) forecasting, which directly influences power markets and unit commitment decisions in power grids. This paper presents a 24-hour ahead GHI forecasting framework for power system applications. The framework leverages a Hybrid Graph-Quantum Neural Network (HGQNN) that effectively captures spatial correlations and quantifies uncertainty—both essential for operational decision-making. Integrating the strength

item.title: Adaptive Polyhedral Approximation for Enhancing the Distributionally Robust Optimization of Uncertain Power Systems
item.description:
Ke Zhang, Xu Wang, M. Shahidehpour, Chuanwen Jiang, Zhaohao Ding
The increasing penetration of renewable energy, particularly wind power, introduces significant uncertainty into power system operations, posing new challenges for reliable and cost-effective dispatch. To address this, we propose a two-stage distributionally robust optimization (DRO) scheduling model that incorporates renewable energy uncertainty using ∞-Wasserstein ambiguity set constructed on unbalanced optimal transport (UOT) augmented empirical data. This approach enhances out-of-sample perf

item.title: Orthogonal Ray Projection: A Tangent-Space Visual Measurement Model for Robust Visual-Inertial Odometry
item.description:
Bing Han, Tuan Li, Yuezu Lv, Weisong Wen, Zhipeng Wang et al.
The reprojection error in Visual-Inertial Odometry (VIO) suffers from high nonlinearity due to perspective division, which degrades estimator consistency and robustness, particularly under large depth uncertainty. To address this, we propose a novel visual measurement model, the Orthogonal Ray Projection Error (ORPE), which is formulated in the tangent space of the observation ray. By minimizing the orthogonal distance between the estimated landmark and the measurement ray, ORPE decouples the me

item.title: A Bi-Level Distributed Framework for Hierarchical Coordination of Multiple Agents’ Flexibility Under Uncertainty of Flexibility Requirements
item.description:
Niloofar Pourghaderi, M. Fotuhi‐Firuzabad, Milad Kabirifar, Mohammad Shahidehpour, Moein Moeini‐Aghtaie
This paper presents a market-based hierarchical framework to coordinate joint energy and flexibility capability trading in a day-ahead market, while considering real-time flexibility adjustment to cover uncertainties in forecasted flexibility requirements, using an adaptive robust optimization (ARO) method. In this regard, a two-stage model is presented; in the first stage, the agents’ energy and flexibility capabilities and the distributed energy resources’ commitments are optimized to meet the

item.title: $\pi$-BA: Probabilistic Neural Bundle Adjustment With Iterative Cycle Optimization for Driving Scene Reconstruction
item.description:
Yunxuan Mao, Dongkun Zhang, Lilu Liu, Yue Wang, Rong Xiong
Urban scene reconstruction under noisy camera poses remains a critical challenge for autonomous driving. While recent neural dense Bundle Adjustment (BA) methods have shown promising results in specific settings, their performance often degrades in real-world urban scenarios due to noisy correspondences and imbalanced optimization between camera poses and scene parameters, which leads the scene representation to overfit to erroneous geometric constraints, causing the system to converge to subopt

item.title: Reliable Federated Multi-View Learning for Heterogeneous Information Fusion in Mobile Edge Computing
item.description:
Daoyuan Li, Zuyuan Yang, Jiawen Kang, Zehui Xiong, Dusist Niyato et al.
The rising data demands of generative AI models, such as large language models (LLMs), underscore the value of utilizing edge device data in mobile computing, where federated learning (FL) provides a privacy-preserving approach via decentralized model training. To address data heterogeneity across edge devices, federated multi-view learning (FedMVL) has been proposed to improve global model performance by capturing consistency and complementarity among diverse data views. However, existing metho

item.title: Federated Deep Reinforcement Learning for Safe Multi-UAV Cooperative Control in IoT Communications
item.description:
Jian Gu, Yin Wang, Wen Ji
The cooperative control of UAVs in a complex environment is the key and fundamental problem in intelligent transportation and IoT communication systems. The cooperative control policy of the UAVs is challenging to determine due to the non-stationarity of the environment caused by the interactions between the UAVs and the environment under IoT communication. The use of deep learning algorithms based on a digital twin (DT) system to optimize the control policy has shown significant benefits in ter

item.title: A Computational Study of a Randomized Semidefinite Programming Approach
item.description:
Maximum Cuts and Fractional Cut Covers
We present experimental work on a primal-dual framework simultaneously approximating maximum cut and weighted fractional cut-covering instances. In this primal-dual framework, we solve a semidefinite programming (SDP) relaxation to either the maximum cut problem or to the weighted fractional cut-covering problem, and then independently sample a collection of cuts via the random-hyperplane technique. We then ... Read more

item.title: Operations research in epidemic-logistics management: from 2012 to 2025
item.description: Zhu, X., Liu, M., Zio, E.

item.title: Cone Ordering in Distributionally Robust Optimization with Set-Valued Probabilities
item.description:
Torre, D., Mendivil, F., Rocca, M.
Abstract
We extend the classical distributionally robust optimization framework by introducing set valued probabilities along with an ordering between sets based on convex, pointed cones where we define
$$\begin{aligned} A \le _C B \quad \iff \quad A \subseteq B - C, \end{aligned}$$
item.source: Journal of Optimization Theory and Applications

item.title: First and Second-Order Conditions for Interval Optimization Problems by Using Generalized Interval Differences
item.description: Chalco-Cano, Y., Hernández-Jiménez, B., Ramos, A.
item.source: Journal of Optimization Theory and Applications

item.title: The Euler-Lagrange and Legendre Necessary Conditions for Fractional Calculus of Variations
item.description: Yusubov, S., Yusubov, S., Mahmudov, E.
item.source: Journal of Optimization Theory and Applications

item.title: Autonomous Navigation for Mobile Robots in Emergency Rescue
item.description:
Hongda Chen, Shanshan Sun, Zefeng Wang, Wei Shen, Jikai Yu et al.
Emergency rescue robots are currently widely used in disaster relief scenarios such as earthquake response, mine collapses, and industrial explosions. Due to environmental challenges such as terrain variability and weak GPS signals, robots experience reduced localization accuracy and prolonged path planning, failing to meet the demands of emergency rescue operations. To address these issues, this study employs the Ackermann quick-response emergency mobile robot as the experimental platform. Init

item.title: Instance-Aware Parameter Configuration in Bilevel Late Acceptance Hill Climbing for the Electric Capacitated Vehicle Routing Problem
item.description:
Yinghao Qin, Xinwei Wang, Mosab Bazargani, Jun Chen
Algorithm performance in combinatorial optimization is highly sensitive to parameter settings, while a single globally tuned configuration often fails to exploit the heterogeneity of instances. This limitation is particularly evident in the Electric Capacitated Vehicle Routing Problem, where instances differ in structure, demand patterns, and energy constraints. This paper investigates instance-aware parameter configuration for Bilevel Late Acceptance Hill Climbing, a state-of-the-art metaheuris
